content.problemStatement:
Initially, Chef is at coordinate 000 on X-axis. For each i=1,2,…,Ni = 1, 2, \dots, Ni=1,2,…,N in order, Chef does the following:


If Chef is at a **non-negative** coordinate, he moves iii steps backward (i.e, his position's coordinate decreases by iii), otherwise he moves iii steps forward (i.e, his position's coordinate increases by iii).
You are given the integer NNN. Find the final position of Chef on the X-axis after NNN operations.


Input Format

The first line of input contains an integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. The TTT test cases then follow:
The first and only line of each test case contains an integer NNN.


Output Format
For each test case, output in a single line the final position of Chef on the X-axis after NNN operations.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the final coordinate of Chef after N operations.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For N=1 (odd) answer = -(1+1)/2 = -1. For N=2 (even) answer = 2/2 = 1. For N=3 (odd) answer = -(3+1)/2 = -2.

solution.approach: Observe the process: starting from 0, after each step the sign of the position flips. For even i the position becomes i/2, for odd i it becomes -(i+1)/2. Hence the final answer is: if N is even, answer = N/2; otherwise answer = -(N+1)/2. This formula is derived by simple induction and yields O(1) time per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.
